name: Braised Whole Fish with Shrimp Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Shao Hsing wine or dry sherry
4 ounces Medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
1 Whole cleaned fish, scaled if necessary
or 1 1/2 pounds Fish fillets or steak
1 tablespoon Shredded or minced fresh ginger
1 tablespoon Soy sauce or bean sauce
1 teaspoon Corn starch mixed with 2 teaspoons water
3 Green onions, thinly sliced on a sharp diagonal (garnish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak mushrooms in warm water until caps are tender, about 15 to 30 minutes; drain.
2. Gently squeeze water from mushrooms, then cut off and discard stems; thinly slice caps. Set aside.
3. In a small bowl, combine 1 tablespoon soy sauce, wine (or sherry), and cornstarch. Add shrimp and stir to coat. Set aside.
4. Lightly sprinkle fish with salt and pepper; dredge in cornstarch and shake off excess or lightly sprinkle with cornstarch. Place a wide frying pan (preferably non-stick) over medium-high heat.
5. Add 1 tablespoon oil to the pan, swirling to coat the surface. Add fish (skin side up if using fillets). Cover and cook until golden brown on the bottom, about 3 minutes.
6. Carefully turn fish over and repeat to brown the other side. Remove fish and set aside.
7. Add the remaining tablespoon of oil to the pan. Add ginger and cook until fragrant, about 10 seconds.
8. Add shrimp and stir until opaque and separated, about 1 minute.
9. Add mushrooms, broth, and soy sauce; cook for 30 seconds.
10. Add cornstarch solution and stir until the sauce boils and thickens.
11. Return fish to the pan and spoon sauce over the fish. Cover and simmer until the center of the thickest part is just opaque, about 3 to 5 minutes.
12. Transfer fish to a platter; pour sauce over fish. Garnish with green onions. Serves 4.
